title: Composition of abstract GJMS operators
summary: Defines ordinary unary-operator composition and associates an ordered composition I with the formal composite P₂I₁∘⋯∘P₂Iᵣ of an abstract GJMS family.

mathematicalContent.heading: Unary composition
mathematicalContent.body: The list [P₁,…,Pᵣ] is folded from the right, so it acts as P₁∘⋯∘Pᵣ; the empty list is the identity.

mathematicalContent.heading: Composition indexed by I
mathematicalContent.body: An abstract GJMS family supplies P₂k for each positive k. A composition I=(I₁,…,Iᵣ) selects and composes those operators in order.

mathematicalContent.heading: Singleton check
mathematicalContent.body: For I=(N), the constructed composition is exactly the single abstract GJMS operator P₂N.

correspondence.leanType: def composeUnary (P Q : Function Conf → Function Conf) : Function Conf → Function Conf
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines ordinary composition of two unary ambient operators.

correspondence.leanType: theorem composeUnary_apply (P Q : Function Conf → Function Conf) (f : Function Conf) : composeUnary P Q f = P (Q f)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Unfolds unary composition on an ambient function.

correspondence.leanType: def idUnary : Function Conf → Function Conf
theorem idUnary_apply (f : Function Conf) : idUnary f = f
def abstractToUnary (P : GJMS.AbstractOperator CalConf) : Function Conf → Function Conf
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Forgets the weight/tangentiality package of an abstract GJMS operator and retains its underlying unary ambient operator.

correspondence.leanType: theorem abstractToUnary_apply (P : GJMS.AbstractOperator CalConf) (f : Function Conf) : abstractToUnary CalConf P f = P.toWeightedTangentialOperator.toOperator f
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Unfolds evaluation of the underlying unary operator.

correspondence.leanType: def composeUnaryList : List (Function Conf → Function Conf) → Function Conf → Function Conf
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Right-folds a list of unary operators into their ordered composition.
The composition of the empty operator list is the identity.

correspondence.leanType: theorem composeUnaryList_cons (P : Function Conf → Function Conf) (Ps : List (Function Conf → Function Conf)) : composeUnaryList (Conf := Conf) (P :: Ps) = composeUnary P (composeUnaryList Ps)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A nonempty list composes its head with the composition of its tail.
Defines an abstract family assigning a GJMS operator to each positive integer k.

correspondence.leanType: def operatorListOfComposition (G : AbstractGJMSFamily CalConf) {N : Nat} (I : Composition N) : List (Function Conf → Function Conf)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Maps the parts of a composition to the corresponding underlying unary GJMS operators.

correspondence.leanType: def composeGJMS (G : AbstractGJMSFamily CalConf) {N : Nat} (I : Composition N) : Function Conf → Function Conf
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the operator composite associated with a composition.

correspondence.leanType: theorem operatorList_singleton (G : AbstractGJMSFamily CalConf) (N : Nat) (hN : 0 < N) : operatorListOfComposition CalConf G (Composition.singleton N hN) = [abstractToUnary CalConf (G N hN)]
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For a singleton composition, the operator list contains exactly G_N.

correspondence.leanType: theorem composeGJMS_singleton (G : AbstractGJMSFamily CalConf) (N : Nat) (hN : 0 < N) : composeGJMS CalConf G (Composition.singleton N hN) = abstractToUnary CalConf (G N hN)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For a singleton composition, the composite equals the underlying operator G_N.

references.note: Source for ordered GJMS compositions and coefficients.

references.locator: Direct ambient proof of Juhl's formulas
references.note: Ambient interpretation of the composition formula.

limitations: This is an operator-composition scaffold. It does not prove conformal covariance of arbitrary composites, compatibility of successive input/output weights, or the Juhl sum identity. The abstract family already carries its component operators as supplied data.
